name: Southwest Cactus Candy
recipeInstructions:
1. Select a prickly pear cactus (or small barrel cactus if you have one, as it is illegal to remove from the desert) and carefully remove spines and outside layer with a large knife.
2. Cut pulp into 1-inch thick slices.
3. Soak overnight in cold water; cut into 1-inch cubes and cook in boiling water until tender.
4. Drain well.
5. Cook slowly in the syrup until nearly all the syrup is absorbed, being careful not to scorch.
6. Heat all ingredients until sugar is dissolved.
7. Add cactus and return it to the syrup.
8. Remove cactus from syrup, drain excess liquid, and roll in granulated or powdered sugar.
9. For colored candy, add vegetable coloring to the syrup before cooking.
